reasons: VIX regime stable — good for weekly holds
Macro data inline with expectations — less directional clarity

entry.instruction: Enter Monday morning. Wait 15-20 min after open for spread to tighten. Limit order at mid (8.35) or better. Skip if gap > 2%.

exit.instruction: Time-decay-aware exits: take 40% day-1 / 35% day-2 / 25% day-3 / 15% day-4 profit. Hard stop at 50% loss. Exit if delta < 0.10. Breakeven requires 4.2% move to $288.35 (weekly expected move: 9.0%).

thesis: DDOG scores highest on the composite (71.1) driven by perfect momentum (100) and near-perfect trend persistence (95), with a fresh analyst upgrade cycle providing a fundamental catalyst that aligns with the technical signal. The mean_reversion score of 75 suggests the move still has room — it's not yet overbought — making this week's entry timing favorable before the trend gets crowded. Kill condition: **DDOG has an earnings flag this week** — if the earnings date falls within the Mon-Fri holding window, this pick must be downgraded or replaced with MRVL (next-best non-semi, but MRVL also has earnings risk and is a 4th semiconductor name, so running only 2 picks may be the disciplined choice).

reasons: VIX regime stable — good for weekly holds
Macro data inline with expectations — less directional clarity

entry.instruction: Enter Monday morning. Wait 15-20 min after open for spread to tighten. Limit order at mid (3.45) or better. Skip if gap > 2%.

exit.instruction: Time-decay-aware exits: take 40% day-1 / 35% day-2 / 25% day-3 / 15% day-4 profit. Hard stop at 50% loss. Exit if delta < 0.10. Breakeven requires 2.1% move to $48.45 (weekly expected move: 11.6%).

thesis: SMCI is driven by momentum (perfect 100) with confirming mean-reversion and trend-persistence scores, a rare triple-alignment that historically signals sustained directional moves. This week is the right entry because SMCI has no earnings exposure during the holding window — unlike AMD, MU, MRVL, and DDOG — meaning the thesis can play out on technicals rather than being overridden by a binary event. Kill condition: if SMCI breaks below its 20-day SMA by >2% on Monday open (gap stop), or if the social "breakdown/risk" flags materialize as a specific negative catalyst (SEC filing issue, customer loss), exit immediately.

reasons: VIX regime stable — good for weekly holds
Macro data inline with expectations — less directional clarity

entry.instruction: Enter Monday morning. Wait 15-20 min after open for spread to tighten. Limit order at mid (0.71) or better. Skip if gap > 2%.

exit.instruction: Time-decay-aware exits: take 40% day-1 / 35% day-2 / 25% day-3 / 15% day-4 profit. Hard stop at 50% loss. Exit if delta < 0.10. Breakeven requires 1.4% move to $17.21 (weekly expected move: 11.4%).

thesis: F is a momentum-driven play (99/100) with strong trend persistence (85) in a sector completely uncorrelated to the semiconductor cluster, giving the portfolio a hedge against tech-specific risk. The entry is attractive now because normal-VIX (15.9) keeps all weight multipliers at baseline — momentum isn't being penalized as it would be in elevated/high regimes — and auto/EV is a sector where idiosyncratic catalysts (tariff news, production data) can drive weekly-scale moves independent of broad tech sentiment. Kill condition: a >2% gap down on Monday open from adverse tariff/trade headline, or if the holding-window high-impact event (Monday) triggers a broad risk-off move that pushes VIX above 20, shifting the regime and deweighting momentum.
